blurb: The Bank of England is the UK's central bank; this covers its research and policy work on artificial intelligence in the financial system.

hiring.note: Official Bank of England careers page; no ATS endpoint found.

publications.title: The Bank of England's approach to innovation in artificial intelligence, distributed ledger technology, and quantum computing
publications.summary: First-party Bank of England publication setting out its strategic approach to enabling responsible innovation across AI, distributed ledger technology (DLT) and quantum computing in the financial system — framing the Bank's role as shaping (not slowing) innovation while remaining vigilant to risks across monetary stability, financial stability and safe-and-sound firms.

publications.title: Financial Stability in Focus: Artificial intelligence in the financial system
publications.summary: Financial Policy Committee analysis of the financial stability implications of growing AI adoption across the financial system.

publications.title: Artificial intelligence in UK financial services - 2024
publications.summary: Joint Bank of England and FCA survey on the use of AI and machine learning in UK financial services and associated benefits and risks.

news.title: Bank of England regulator expects 'quite significant disruption' from latest AI models
news.summary: The head of the Bank of England's regulatory arm (PRA) said it is 'reasonable to expect quite significant disruption' to financial services from the latest AI models such as Anthropic's Mythos and ChatGPT 5.5 Instant — a notable financial-stability framing of frontier-model risk. Reuters; ties to the BoE's macroprudential AI programme.

news.title: Bank of England response to TSC inquiry report on AI in financial services
news.summary: The Bank's formal response to the Treasury Select Committee's inquiry on AI in financial services; the PRA has highlighted AI adoption in its 2026 supervisory priorities and is running a macroprudential programme on AI's system-level impact. First-party BoE document.

notes: Regulator/central bank research site. Dates taken from each page's first-party datePublished field. Innovation-approach report datePublished=2026-07-02 (published today; feed ceiling had been ~15mo stale at 2025-04-09 despite an active AI programme per news[]); a related 2026-06-30 dateline also appears on the page but is a secondary/linked item, not this report. Earlier: 09 April 2025 (FSiF AI); 21 November 2024 (BoE/FCA AI survey 2024).
